events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Sir Richard Starkey, known as Ringo Starr, was born on July 7, 1940, and rose to international fame as the drummer for The Beatles.

angles.name: Drumming Legend
angles.copy: Eighty-six today: the legendary Ringo Starr! From his unique drumming style to his iconic vocals on tracks like 'Yellow Submarine', what's your favourite Ringo moment?

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Sir Brian Harold May, born on July 19, 1947, is the iconic lead guitarist and co-founder of the rock band Queen, also known for his work as an astrophysicist.

angles.name: Guitar God & Astrophysicist
angles.copy: Happy 79th birthday to the one and only Brian May! Not just a guitar legend with Queen, but an astrophysicist too. What's your top Queen track featuring his incredible Red Special?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Sir Michael Philip Jagger, born on July 26, 1943, is the charismatic lead singer and a founding member of The Rolling Stones, a career spanning more than six decades.

angles.name: Ageless Rock Icon
angles.copy: Still got the moves at 83! Happy birthday to the one and only Mick Jagger. What's your favourite Rolling Stones anthem that still gets you moving?

events.title: MTV launched (1981)
events.blurb: On August 1, 1981, MTV launched in the United States, famously kicking off with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star', forever changing the music landscape.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: Remember when music videos changed everything? It was 45 years ago today that MTV launched, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its very first clip. The irony!

events.title: Oasis released Definitely Maybe (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis' debut album, 'Definitely Maybe', was released on August 29, 1994, becoming a pivotal record in the Britpop movement and one of the fastest-selling debut albums in UK history.

angles.name: Britpop Milestone
angles.copy: Thirty-two years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe'. That album defined a generation. What's your standout track from their iconic debut?

events.title: Woodstock Festival took place (1969)
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, billed as 'An Aquarian Exposition: 3 Days of Peace & Music', was held from August 15-18, 1969, on a dairy farm in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Peace & Music
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago today, the iconic Woodstock Festival began. Three days of peace, music, and a lineup that changed history. Which legendary performance do you wish you'd seen live?

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's passing
events.blurb: Elvis Presley, who would have been 91, passed away on August 16, 1977, leaving behind an unparalleled legacy as 'The King of Rock and Roll'.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: Remembering The King today. Elvis Presley, who would have been 91, left us on this day in 1977, but his music still reigns supreme. What's your all-time favourite Elvis hit?

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur's passing
events.blurb: Tupac Shakur, who would have been 55, tragically passed away on September 1, 1996, leaving an indelible mark on hip-hop and popular culture.

angles.name: Tupac's Legacy
angles.copy: Thirty years since we lost a true icon. Remembering Tupac Shakur today, who would have been 55. His lyrics and impact continue to resonate. What's the Tupac track that means the most to you?

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's passing
events.blurb: Jimi Hendrix, who would have been 84, passed away on September 2, 1970, forever remembered as one of the most influential guitarists in history.

angles.name: Guitar Innovator
angles.copy: Remembering the guitar genius, Jimi Hendrix, who would have been 84. On this day in 1970, the music world lost a true innovator. What's your favourite Hendrix riff?

events.title: The Beatles released Abbey Road (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles' iconic 'Abbey Road' album was released in the UK on September 26, 1969, featuring its legendary zebra crossing cover.

angles.name: Iconic Album & Cover
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago today, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road' – an album as iconic as its cover. What's the first track you think of when you see that legendary crosswalk?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Christine Ellen Hynde, born on September 7, 1951, is the distinctive voice and founding member of The Pretenders.

angles.name: Pretenders Frontwoman
angles.copy: Wishing a very happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! Her powerful voice and songwriting with The Pretenders have given us so many classics. Which one's your favourite?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: William John Paul Gallagher, born on September 21, 1972, is the distinctive lead singer of Oasis and a prominent figure in British rock music.

angles.name: Rock 'n' Roll Star
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! The voice of a generation and a true rock 'n' roll star. What's your favourite Liam moment, musical or otherwise?

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' released (1975 UK Single)
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic single 'Imagine' was released in the UK on October 1, 1975, as part of the 'Shaved Fish' compilation, having originally been released in the US in 1971.

angles.name: A Song For Peace
angles.copy: Fifty-one years since John Lennon gave us the timeless message of 'Imagine'. It’s a song that still resonates globally. What does 'Imagine' mean to you?

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: The first-generation Apple iPod was announced on October 23, 2001, and became available on November 10, 2001, revolutionising how we carry our music.

angles.name: Music in Your Pocket
angles.copy: Remember life before the iPod? Twenty-five years ago, Apple introduced the first iPod, putting '1,000 songs in your pocket' and changing music consumption forever. Did you get one of the originals?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: James Bond Day is celebrated annually on October 5th, marking the release date of the very first James Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Sixty-four years since 'Dr. No' hit screens, giving us the world's most iconic spy. What's your favourite Bond film or theme song?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: John Lennon, born on October 9, 1940, would have been 86 today. The legendary singer-songwriter and peace activist was a founding member of The Beatles.

angles.name: Lennon's Enduring Spirit
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we remember the unparalleled talent and enduring spirit of John Lennon. His music and message continue to inspire. What's your favourite Lennon lyric?
